revision.comment: 新規

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 岡太神社 |画像 = [[File:Okata-jinja-Nishinomiya.jpg|300px]]狛犬ではなく、災害を静止（しし）する猪像 |所在地 = 兵庫県西宮市小松南町2-2-8 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.1|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]] |社格 = 延喜式内社、旧県社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 =岡司宮（おかしの宮） |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月11日]] 一時上臈 |神事 = }}
'''岡大神社'''（おかたじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]小松南町に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[武庫郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]。
== 祭神 == * [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]
=== 相殿 === * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚神 ]]・[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫神]]・[[大国主|大己貴神]]・[[蘇民将来]]
== 歴史 == 社伝に、[[宇多天皇]][[寛平]]5年（[[893年]]）[[武庫郡]]広田の人岡司新五氏がこの地を開発して浜村と称し、[[延喜]]元年（[[901年]]）広田明神を祀り五穀豊穣を得たという。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、本社の北の元上の宮5柱を合祀<ref>式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』５（1977）{{全国書誌番号|78005760}}</ref>。 * 明治6年（[[1873年]]）8月村社に列す。 * 明治12年（[[1879年]]）4月[[近代社格制度|県社]]に昇格<ref>明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）</ref>。 * [[平成]]7年（[[1995年]]）1月17日の[[阪神・淡路大震災]]で社殿倒壊。 * 平成12年（[[2000年]]）再建。
== 境内 == * 恵美須社 祭神：[[えびす|恵美須大神]] * 九重供養塔 伝小松内府平重盛卿供養塔 * [[平重盛]]之城址 岡太社の西南「伏松」という小高い丘が城趾と伝承されている。 * 石標 従是東尼崎領 [[尼崎藩]]領界碑 * 岡太神社社号標石 岡太社 小松村 同志菅広房 [[元文]]6年（[[1741年]]）建立。 [[並河誠所]]が[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）『[[五畿内志]]』「摂津志」に「岡太神社小松村にあり」と記し、社号標石を設置して式内社と比定した。[[廣田神社]]境外摂社で[[神戸女学院大学]]構内に鎮座する岡田神社がもう一つの論社となっている。
== 交通アクセス == * [[阪神本線|阪神]] [[福知山線]][[武庫川駅]]より、北西に徒歩6分
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:西宮市の神社]]

== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: 鳴尾村誌をもとに加筆
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 岡太神社 |画像 = [[File:Okata-jinja-Nishinomiya.jpg|300px]]狛犬ではなく、災害を静止（しし）する猪像 |所在地 = 兵庫県西宮市小松南町2-2-8 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.1|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]] |社格 = 延喜式内社、旧県社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 =岡司宮（おかしの宮） |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月11日]] [[#一時上臈]] |神事 = }}
'''岡大神社'''（おかたじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]小松南町に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[武庫郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の論社となっている。[[鳴尾]]地域では最古の神社である<ref name="鳴尾46">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.46。</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]
=== 相殿 === * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚神 ]]・[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫神]]・[[大国主|大己貴神]]・[[蘇民将来]]
== 歴史 == 社伝に、[[宇多天皇]][[寛平]]5年（[[893年]]）[[武庫郡]]広田の人岡司新五氏がこの地を開発して浜村と称し、[[延喜]]元年（[[901年]]）広田明神を祀り五穀豊穣を得たという<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.47。</ref>。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、本社の北の元上の宮5柱を合祀<ref>式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5（1977）{{全国書誌番号|78005760}}</ref>。 * 明治6年（[[1873年]]）8月村社に列す。 * 明治12年（[[1879年]]）4月[[近代社格制度|県社]]に昇格<ref>明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）</ref>。 * [[平成]]7年（[[1995年]]）1月17日の[[阪神・淡路大震災]]で社殿倒壊。 * 平成12年（[[2000年]]）再建。
=== 異称 === 「おかしの宮」という異称があるが、この由来としてはいくつかの説がある。 *上述のように、「岡司」氏が開いたことによる<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.48。</ref>。 *武庫行宮にあたる「押照宮」から転じた（ただし、武庫行宮の比定地は別地点が有力である）<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、pp.49-50。</ref>。 *後述するように、女装して供物を奉納する祭りが行われており、それが「おかし」と評されたことによる<ref name="鳴尾52">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.52。</ref>。
== 境内 == * 恵美須社 祭神：[[えびす|恵美須大神]] * 九重供養塔 伝小松内府平重盛卿供養塔 * [[平重盛]]之城址 岡太社の西南「伏松」という小高い丘が城趾と伝承されている。 * 石標 従是東尼崎領 [[尼崎藩]]領界碑 * 岡太神社社号標石 岡太社 小松村 同志菅広房 [[元文]]6年（[[1741年]]）建立。 [[並河誠所]]が[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）『[[五畿内志]]』「摂津志」に「岡太神社小松村にあり」と記し、社号標石を設置して式内社と比定した。[[廣田神社]]境外摂社で[[神戸女学院大学]]構内に鎮座する岡田神社がもう一つの論社となっている。なお、両社とも[[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]を祀っているなど、両社に何かしらの関連があるという見方もある<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.49。</ref>。
== 祭礼 == === 一時上臈 === 毎年[[10月11日]]には、南北の講である「頭屋」が男女のヒトガタである[[御幣]]を作り、[[神饌]]とともに奉納される<ref name="鳴尾51">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.51。</ref>。かつては頭屋から神社まで奉納行列が行われていたが、新住民の流入でコミュニティが希薄化し、さらには[[阪神・淡路大震災]]がとどめを刺した形となり<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.54。</ref>、平成9年を最後に奉納行列は行われなくなった<ref name="鳴尾51" />。
[[江戸時代]]には、その年に小松へ嫁いだ女性の衣装を男が着て供物を奉納する形で行われていたというが<ref name="鳴尾52" />、これに付随して[[岩見重太郎]]が[[狒々]]を退治したとする伝説が残っている<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.53。</ref>。
=== 静止打 === [[1月9日 (旧暦)|陰暦正月9日]]に行われていた。この日は[[西宮神社]]の[[ヒルコ|蛭児神]]が静止（しし）を打つ{{refnest|group="*"|「災害を事前に防ぎ、五穀豊穣をもたらす」の意<ref name="鳴尾73">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.73。</ref>。}}日であり、それを避けるために[[等覚寺 (西宮市)|等覚寺]]へ逃れた[[イノシシ|猪]]（しし）を迎えに行く祭礼であった<ref name="鳴尾73" />。のちに途絶しているが、神社の境内には、現在も猪の像が鎮座している。
== 交通アクセス == * [[阪神本線]][[武庫川駅]]より、北西に徒歩6分 *旧国道（[[中国街道]]）に面する<ref name="鳴尾46" />。
== 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="*"}}
=== 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|1=和書|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=西宮市鳴尾区有財産管理委員会|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 岡太神社 |画像 = [[File:Okata-jinja-Nishinomiya.jpg|300px]]狛犬ではなく、災害を静止（しし）する猪像 |所在地 = 兵庫県西宮市小松南町2-2-8 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.1|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]] |社格 = 延喜式内社、旧県社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 =岡司宮（おかしの宮） |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月11日]] [[#一時上臈]] |神事 = }}
'''岡大神社'''（おかたじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]小松南町に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[武庫郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の論社となっている。[[鳴尾]]地域では最古の神社である<ref name="鳴尾46">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.46。</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]
=== 相殿 === * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚神 ]]・[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫神]]・[[大国主|大己貴神]]・[[蘇民将来]]
== 歴史 == 社伝に、[[宇多天皇]][[寛平]]5年（[[893年]]）[[武庫郡]]広田の人岡司新五氏がこの地を開発して浜村と称し、[[延喜]]元年（[[901年]]）広田明神を祀り五穀豊穣を得たという<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.47。</ref>。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）、本社の北の元上の宮5柱を合祀<ref>式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5（1977）{{全国書誌番号|78005760}}</ref>。 * 明治6年（[[1873年]]）8月村社に列す。 * 明治12年（[[1879年]]）4月[[近代社格制度|県社]]に昇格<ref>明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）</ref>。 * [[平成]]7年（[[1995年]]）1月17日の[[阪神・淡路大震災]]で社殿倒壊。 * 平成12年（[[2000年]]）再建。
=== 異称 === 「おかしの宮」という異称があるが、この由来としてはいくつかの説がある。 *上述のように、「岡司」氏が開いたことによる<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.48。</ref>。 *武庫行宮にあたる「押照宮」から転じた（ただし、武庫行宮の比定地は別地点が有力である）<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、pp.49-50。</ref>。 *後述するように、女装して供物を奉納する祭りが行われており、それが「おかし」と評されたことによる<ref name="鳴尾52">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.52。</ref>。
== 境内 == * 恵美須社 祭神：[[えびす|恵美須大神]] * 九重供養塔 伝小松内府平重盛卿供養塔 * [[平重盛]]之城址 岡太社の西南「伏松」という小高い丘が城趾と伝承されている。 * 石標 従是東尼崎領 [[尼崎藩]]領界碑 * 岡太神社社号標石 岡太社 小松村 同志菅広房 [[元文]]6年（[[1741年]]）建立。 [[並河誠所]]が[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）『[[五畿内志]]』「摂津志」に「岡太神社小松村にあり」と記し、社号標石を設置して式内社と比定した。[[廣田神社]]境外摂社で[[神戸女学院大学]]構内に鎮座する岡田神社がもう一つの論社となっている。なお、両社とも[[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]を祀っているなど、両社に何かしらの関連があるという見方もある<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.49。</ref>。
== 祭礼 == === 一時上臈 === 毎年[[10月11日]]には、南北の講である「頭屋」が男女のヒトガタである[[御幣]]を作り、[[神饌]]とともに奉納される<ref name="鳴尾51">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.51。</ref>。かつては頭屋から神社まで奉納行列が行われていたが、新住民の流入でコミュニティが希薄化し、さらには[[阪神・淡路大震災]]がとどめを刺した形となり<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.54。</ref>、平成9年を最後に奉納行列は行われなくなった<ref name="鳴尾51" />。
[[江戸時代]]には、その年に小松へ嫁いだ女性の衣装を男が着て供物を奉納する形で行われていたというが<ref name="鳴尾52" />、これに付随して[[岩見重太郎]]が[[狒々]]を退治したとする伝説が残っている<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.53。</ref>。
=== 静止打 === [[1月9日 (旧暦)|陰暦正月9日]]に行われていた。この日は[[西宮神社]]の[[ヒルコ|蛭児神]]が静止（しし）を打つ{{refnest|group="*"|「災害を事前に防ぎ、五穀豊穣をもたらす」の意<ref name="鳴尾73">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.73。</ref>。}}日であり、それを避けるために[[等覚寺 (西宮市)|等覚寺]]へ逃れた[[イノシシ|猪]]（しし）を迎えに行く祭礼であった<ref name="鳴尾73" />。のちに途絶しているが、神社の境内には、現在も猪の像が鎮座している。
== 交通アクセス == * [[阪神本線]][[武庫川駅]]より、北西に徒歩6分 *旧国道（[[中国街道]]）に面する<ref name="鳴尾46" />。

=== 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 岡太神社 |画像 = [[File:Okata-jinja-Nishinomiya.jpg|300px]]狛犬ではなく、災害を静止（しし）する猪像 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]小松南町二丁目2-8 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]] |社格 = 延喜式内社、旧県社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 岡司宮（おかしの宮） |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月11日]] [[#一時上臈]] |神事 = }}
[[File:2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|社殿 両脇には狛犬が鎮座する。]] '''岡太神社'''（おかたじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]小松南町に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[武庫郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の論社となっている。[[鳴尾]]地域では最古の神社である<ref name="鳴尾46">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.46。</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]
=== 相殿 === * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚神 ]]・[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫神]]・[[大国主|大己貴神]]・[[蘇民将来]]
== 歴史 == 社伝に、[[宇多天皇]][[寛平]]5年（[[893年]]）[[武庫郡]]広田の人岡司新五氏がこの地を開発して浜村と称し、[[延喜]]元年（[[901年]]）広田明神を祀り五穀豊穣を得たという<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.47。</ref>。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]） - 本社の北の元上の宮5柱を合祀<ref>式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5（1977）{{全国書誌番号|78005760}}</ref>。 * 明治6年（[[1873年]]）8月 - 村社に列す。 * 明治12年（[[1879年]]）4月 - [[近代社格制度|県社]]に昇格<ref>明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）</ref>。 * [[平成]]7年（[[1995年]]）[[1月17日]] - [[阪神・淡路大震災]]で社殿倒壊。 * 平成12年（[[2000年]]） - 再建。
=== 異称 === 「おかしの宮」という異称があるが、この由来としてはいくつかの説がある。 *上述のように、「岡司」氏が開いたことによる<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.48。</ref>。 *武庫行宮にあたる「押照宮」から転じた（ただし、武庫行宮の比定地は別地点が有力である）<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、pp.49-50。</ref>。 *後述するように、女装して供物を奉納する祭りが行われており、それが「おかし」と評されたことによる<ref name="鳴尾52">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.52。</ref>。
== 境内 == [[File:2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shagohyo.jpg|thumb|200px|社号標石]] [[File:2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Ebisu.jpg|thumb|200px|恵美須社]] * 社号標石 - 「岡太社」「小松村」「同志菅広房」と刻銘。 [[元文]]6年（[[1741年]]）建立。 ** [[並河誠所]]が[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）『[[五畿内志]]』の『摂津志』に「岡太神社小松村にあり」と記し、社号標石を設置して式内社と比定した。[[廣田神社]]境外摂社で[[神戸女学院大学]]構内に鎮座する岡田神社がもう一つの論社となっている。なお、両社とも[[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]を祀っているなど、両社に何かしらの関連があるという見方もある<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.49。</ref>。 * 恵美須社 - 社殿左奥に鎮座。祭神：[[えびす|恵美須大神]] * 九重供養塔（伝小松内府平重盛卿供養塔） * [[平重盛]]之城址 - 当社の西南「伏松」という小高い丘が城趾と伝承されている。 * [[尼崎藩]]領界碑 - 石標。「従是東尼崎領」など刻銘。
<gallery> 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|猪像（吽形）<!--阿・吽の順に並べると「二頭」がページ上でそっぽを向いてしまうため、意図的に逆としています。--> 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|猪像（阿形） 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Kuyoto.jpg|九重供養塔 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Heike-Shiroato.jpg|平重盛之城址 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|尼崎藩領界碑 </gallery>
== 祭礼 == === 一時上臈 === 毎年[[10月11日]]には、南北の講である「頭屋」が男女のヒトガタである[[御幣]]を作り、[[神饌]]とともに奉納される<ref name="鳴尾51">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.51。</ref>。かつては頭屋から神社まで奉納行列が行われていたが、新住民の流入でコミュニティが希薄化し、さらには[[阪神・淡路大震災]]がとどめを刺した形となり<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.54。</ref>、平成9年を最後に奉納行列は行われなくなった<ref name="鳴尾51" />。
[[江戸時代]]には、その年に小松へ嫁いだ女性の衣装を男が着て供物を奉納する形で行われていたというが<ref name="鳴尾52" />、これに付随して[[岩見重太郎]]が[[狒々]]を退治したとする伝説が残っている<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.53。</ref>。
=== 静止打 === [[1月9日 (旧暦)|陰暦正月9日]]に行われていた。この日は[[西宮神社]]の[[ヒルコ|蛭児神]]が静止（しし）を打つ{{refnest|group="*"|「災害を事前に防ぎ、五穀豊穣をもたらす」の意<ref name="鳴尾73">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.73。</ref>。}}日であり、それを避けるために[[等覚寺 (西宮市)|等覚寺]]へ逃れた[[イノシシ|猪]]（しし）を迎えに行く祭礼であった<ref name="鳴尾73" />。のちに途絶しているが、神社の境内には、現在も猪の像が鎮座している。
== 交通アクセス == * [[武庫川駅|阪神武庫川駅]]より、北西へ徒歩約6分<!--当駅には武庫川線もあるため、本線のみの表記を削除します。--> * 旧国道（[[中国街道]]）に面する<ref name="鳴尾46" />。
== 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="*"}}
=== 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|1=和書|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=西宮市鳴尾区有財産管理委員会|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]]

=== 異称 === 「おかしの宮」という異称があるが、この由来としてはいくつかの説がある。 *上述のように、「岡司」氏が開いたことによる<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.48。</ref>。 *武庫行宮にあたる「押照宮」から転じた（ただし、武庫行宮の比定地は別地点が有力である）<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、pp.49-50。</ref>。 *後述するように、女装して供物を奉納する祭りが行われており、それが「おかし」と評されたことによる<ref name="鳴尾52">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.52。</ref>。

<gallery> 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|猪像（吽形）<!--阿・吽の順に並べると「二頭」がページ上でそっぽを向いてしまうため、意図的に逆としています。--> 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|猪像（阿形） 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Kuyoto.jpg|九重供養塔 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Heike-Shiroato.jpg|平重盛之城址 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|尼崎藩領界碑 </gallery>
== 祭礼 == === 一時上臈 === 毎年[[10月11日]]には、南北の講である「頭屋」が男女のヒトガタである[[御幣]]を作り、[[神饌]]とともに奉納される<ref name="鳴尾51">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.51。</ref>。かつては頭屋から神社まで奉納行列が行われていたが、新住民の流入でコミュニティが希薄化し、さらには[[阪神・淡路大震災]]がとどめを刺した形となり<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.54。</ref>、平成9年を最後に奉納行列は行われなくなった<ref name="鳴尾51" />。
[[江戸時代]]には、その年に小松へ嫁いだ女性の衣装を男が着て供物を奉納する形で行われていたというが<ref name="鳴尾52" />、これに付随して[[岩見重太郎]]が[[狒々]]を退治したとする伝説が残っている<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.53。</ref>。
=== 静止打 === [[1月9日 (旧暦)|陰暦正月9日]]に行われていた。この日は[[西宮神社]]の[[ヒルコ|蛭児神]]が静止（しし）を打つ{{refnest|group="*"|「災害を事前に防ぎ、五穀豊穣をもたらす」の意<ref name="鳴尾73">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.73。</ref>。}}日であり、それを避けるために[[等覚寺 (西宮市)|等覚寺]]へ逃れた[[イノシシ|猪]]（しし）を迎えに行く祭礼であった<ref name="鳴尾73" />。のちに途絶しているが、神社の境内には、現在も猪の像が鎮座している。
== 交通アクセス == * [[武庫川駅|阪神武庫川駅]]より、北西へ徒歩約6分<!--当駅には武庫川線もあるため、本線のみの表記を削除します。--> * 旧国道（[[中国街道]]）に面する<ref name="鳴尾46" />。
== 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="*"}}
=== 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|1=和書|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=西宮市鳴尾区有財産管理委員会|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]]

revision.comment: 地図記載、座標情報追記
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 岡太神社 |画像 = [[File:Okata-jinja-Nishinomiya.jpg|300px]]狛犬ではなく、災害を静止（しし）する猪像 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]小松南町二丁目2-8 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]] |社格 = 延喜式内社、旧県社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 岡司宮（おかしの宮） |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月11日]] [[#一時上臈]] |神事 = |地図={{Location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|label=岡太神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}
[[File:2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|社殿 両脇には狛犬が鎮座する。]] '''岡太神社'''（おかたじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]小松南町に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[武庫郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の論社となっている。[[鳴尾]]地域では最古の神社である<ref name="鳴尾46">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.46。</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]
=== 相殿 === * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚神 ]]・[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫神]]・[[大国主|大己貴神]]・[[蘇民将来]]
== 歴史 == 社伝に、[[宇多天皇]][[寛平]]5年（[[893年]]）[[武庫郡]]広田の人岡司新五氏がこの地を開発して浜村と称し、[[延喜]]元年（[[901年]]）広田明神を祀り五穀豊穣を得たという<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.47。</ref>。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]） - 本社の北の元上の宮5柱を合祀<ref>式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5（1977）{{全国書誌番号|78005760}}</ref>。 * 明治6年（[[1873年]]）8月 - 村社に列す。 * 明治12年（[[1879年]]）4月 - [[近代社格制度|県社]]に昇格<ref>明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）</ref>。 * [[平成]]7年（[[1995年]]）[[1月17日]] - [[阪神・淡路大震災]]で社殿倒壊。 * 平成12年（[[2000年]]） - 再建。
=== 異称 === 「おかしの宮」という異称があるが、この由来としてはいくつかの説がある。 *上述のように、「岡司」氏が開いたことによる<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.48。</ref>。 *武庫行宮にあたる「押照宮」から転じた（ただし、武庫行宮の比定地は別地点が有力である）<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、pp.49-50。</ref>。 *後述するように、女装して供物を奉納する祭りが行われており、それが「おかし」と評されたことによる<ref name="鳴尾52">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.52。</ref>。
== 境内 == [[File:2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shagohyo.jpg|thumb|200px|社号標石]] [[File:2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Ebisu.jpg|thumb|200px|恵美須社]] * 社号標石 - 「岡太社」「小松村」「同志菅広房」と刻銘。 [[元文]]6年（[[1741年]]）建立。 ** [[並河誠所]]が[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）『[[五畿内志]]』の『摂津志』に「岡太神社小松村にあり」と記し、社号標石を設置して式内社と比定した。[[廣田神社]]境外摂社で[[神戸女学院大学]]構内に鎮座する岡田神社がもう一つの論社となっている。なお、両社とも[[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]を祀っているなど、両社に何かしらの関連があるという見方もある<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.49。</ref>。 * 恵美須社 - 社殿左奥に鎮座。祭神：[[えびす|恵美須大神]] * 九重供養塔（伝小松内府平重盛卿供養塔） * [[平重盛]]之城址 - 当社の西南「伏松」という小高い丘が城趾と伝承されている。 * [[尼崎藩]]領界碑 - 石標。「従是東尼崎領」など刻銘。
<gallery> 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|猪像（吽形）<!--阿・吽の順に並べると「二頭」がページ上でそっぽを向いてしまうため、意図的に逆としています。--> 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|猪像（阿形） 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Kuyoto.jpg|九重供養塔 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Heike-Shiroato.jpg|平重盛之城址 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|尼崎藩領界碑 </gallery>
== 祭礼 == === 一時上臈 === 毎年[[10月11日]]には、南北の講である「頭屋」が男女のヒトガタである[[御幣]]を作り、[[神饌]]とともに奉納される<ref name="鳴尾51">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.51。</ref>。かつては頭屋から神社まで奉納行列が行われていたが、新住民の流入でコミュニティが希薄化し、さらには[[阪神・淡路大震災]]がとどめを刺した形となり<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.54。</ref>、平成9年を最後に奉納行列は行われなくなった<ref name="鳴尾51" />。
[[江戸時代]]には、その年に小松へ嫁いだ女性の衣装を男が着て供物を奉納する形で行われていたというが<ref name="鳴尾52" />、これに付随して[[岩見重太郎]]が[[狒々]]を退治したとする伝説が残っている<ref>『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.53。</ref>。
=== 静止打 === [[1月9日 (旧暦)|陰暦正月9日]]に行われていた。この日は[[西宮神社]]の[[ヒルコ|蛭児神]]が静止（しし）を打つ{{refnest|group="*"|「災害を事前に防ぎ、五穀豊穣をもたらす」の意<ref name="鳴尾73">『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.73。</ref>。}}日であり、それを避けるために[[等覚寺 (西宮市)|等覚寺]]へ逃れた[[イノシシ|猪]]（しし）を迎えに行く祭礼であった<ref name="鳴尾73" />。のちに途絶しているが、神社の境内には、現在も猪の像が鎮座している。
== 交通アクセス == * [[武庫川駅|阪神武庫川駅]]より、北西へ徒歩約6分<!--当駅には武庫川線もあるため、本線のみの表記を削除します。--> * 旧国道（[[中国街道]]）に面する<ref name="鳴尾46" />。
== 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="*"}}
=== 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|1=和書|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=西宮市鳴尾区有財産管理委員会|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]]

境内鳥居（左右に猪像） |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]小松南町二丁目2-8 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）論社
旧[[県社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 岡司宮（おかしの宮） |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月11日]] [[#一時上臈]] |神事 = |地図={{Location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}}}} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''岡太神社'''（おかたじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]小松南町にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]論社で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。[[鳴尾]]地域では最古の神社とされる『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.46。。 == 祭神 == * [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]] === 相殿 === * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚神 ]]・[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫神]]・[[大国主|大己貴神]]・[[蘇民将来]] == 歴史 == 社伝に、[[宇多天皇]][[寛平]]5年（[[893年]]）[[武庫郡]]広田の人岡司新五氏がこの地を開発して浜村と称し、[[延喜]]元年（[[901年]]）広田明神を祀り五穀豊穣を得たという『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.47。。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]） - 本社の北の元上の宮5柱を合祀式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5（1977）{{全国書誌番号|78005760}}。 * 明治6年（[[1873年]]）8月 - 村社に列す。 * 明治12年（[[1879年]]）4月 - [[近代社格制度|県社]]に昇格明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）。 * [[平成]]7年（[[1995年]]）[[1月17日]] - [[阪神・淡路大震災]]で社殿倒壊。 * 平成12年（[[2000年]]） - 再建。 === 異称 === 「おかしの宮」という異称があるが、この由来としてはいくつかの説がある。 *上述のように、「岡司」氏が開いたことによる『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.48。。 *武庫行宮にあたる「押照宮」から転じた（ただし、武庫行宮の比定地は別地点が有力である）『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、pp.49-50。。 *後述するように、女装して供物を奉納する祭りが行われており、それが「おかし」と評されたことによる『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.52。。 == 境内 == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|社殿}}]] * 社号標石 - 「岡太社」「小松村」「同志菅広房」と刻銘。 [[元文]]6年（[[1741年]]）建立。 ** [[並河誠所]]が[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）『[[五畿内志]]』の『摂津志』に「岡太神社小松村にあり」と記し、社号標石を設置して式内社と比定した。[[廣田神社]]境外摂社で[[神戸女学院大学]]構内に鎮座する岡田神社がもう一つの論社となっている。なお、両社とも[[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]を祀っているなど、両社に何かしらの関連があるという見方もある『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.49。。 * 恵美須社 - 社殿左奥に鎮座。祭神：[[えびす|恵美須大神]] * 九重供養塔（伝小松内府平重盛卿供養塔） * [[平重盛]]之城址 - 当社の西南「伏松」という小高い丘が城趾と伝承されている。 * [[尼崎藩]]領界碑 - 石標。「従是東尼崎領」など刻銘。 Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|「岡太社」社号標 Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|恵比須社 Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|九重供養塔（伝平重盛供養塔） Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|平重盛之城趾碑 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|尼崎藩領界碑 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|猪像（吽形） 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|猪像（阿形） == 祭礼 == === 一時上臈 === 毎年[[10月11日]]には、南北の講である「頭屋」が男女のヒトガタである[[御幣]]を作り、[[神饌]]とともに奉納される『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.51。。かつては頭屋から神社まで奉納行列が行われていたが、新住民の流入でコミュニティが希薄化し、さらには[[阪神・淡路大震災]]がとどめを刺した形となり『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.54。、平成9年を最後に奉納行列は行われなくなった。 [[江戸時代]]には、その年に小松へ嫁いだ女性の衣装を男が着て供物を奉納する形で行われていたというが、これに付随して[[岩見重太郎]]が[[狒々]]を退治したとする伝説が残っている『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.53。。 === 静止打 === [[1月9日 (旧暦)|陰暦正月9日]]に行われていた。この日は[[西宮神社]]の[[ヒルコ|蛭児神]]が静止（しし）を打つ{{refnest|group="*"|「災害を事前に防ぎ、五穀豊穣をもたらす」の意『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.73。。}}日であり、それを避けるために[[等覚寺 (西宮市)|等覚寺]]へ逃れた[[イノシシ|猪]]（しし）を迎えに行く祭礼であった。のちに途絶しているが、神社の境内には、現在も猪の像が鎮座している。 == 文化財 == === 西宮市指定文化財 === * 無形民俗文化財 ** 岡太神社一時上臈 - 1984年（昭和59年）3月23日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf 西宮市内の指定文化財一覧]}}（西宮市ホームページ）。。 == 交通アクセス == * [[武庫川駅|阪神武庫川駅]]より、北西へ徒歩約6分 * 旧国道（[[中国街道]]）に面する。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="*"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|1=和書|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=西宮市鳴尾区有財産管理委員会|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 岡太神社 |画像 = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
境内鳥居（左右に猪像） |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]小松南町二丁目2-8 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）論社
旧[[県社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 岡司宮（おかしの宮） |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月11日]] [[#一時上臈]] |神事 = |地図={{Location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}}}} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''岡太神社'''（おかたじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]小松南町にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]論社で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。[[鳴尾]]地域では最古の神社といわれる『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.46。。 == 祭神 == * [[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]] === 相殿 === * [[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞嗚神 ]]・[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫神]]・[[大国主|大己貴神]]・[[蘇民将来]] == 歴史 == 社伝に、[[宇多天皇]][[寛平]]5年（[[893年]]）[[武庫郡]]広田の人岡司新五氏がこの地を開発して浜村と称し、[[延喜]]元年（[[901年]]）広田明神を祀り五穀豊穣を得たという『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.47。。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]） - 本社の北の元上の宮5柱を合祀式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5（1977）{{全国書誌番号|78005760}}。 * 明治6年（[[1873年]]）8月 - 村社に列す。 * 明治12年（[[1879年]]）4月 - [[近代社格制度|県社]]に昇格明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）。 * [[平成]]7年（[[1995年]]）[[1月17日]] - [[阪神・淡路大震災]]で社殿倒壊。 * 平成12年（[[2000年]]） - 再建。 === 異称 === 「おかしの宮」という異称があるが、この由来としてはいくつかの説がある。 *上述のように、「岡司」氏が開いたことによる『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.48。。 *武庫行宮にあたる「押照宮」から転じた（ただし、武庫行宮の比定地は別地点が有力である）『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、pp.49-50。。 *後述するように、女装して供物を奉納する祭りが行われており、それが「おかし」と評されたことによる『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.52。。 == 境内 == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|社殿}}]] * 社号標石 - 「岡太社」「小松村」「同志菅広房」と刻銘。 [[元文]]6年（[[1741年]]）建立。 ** [[並河誠所]]が[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）『[[五畿内志]]』の『摂津志』に「岡太神社小松村にあり」と記し、社号標石を設置して式内社と比定した。[[廣田神社]]境外摂社で[[神戸女学院大学]]構内に鎮座する岡田神社がもう一つの論社となっている。なお、両社とも[[天之御中主神|天御中主大神]]を祀っているなど、両社に何かしらの関連があるという見方もある『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.49。。 * 恵美須社 - 社殿左奥に鎮座。祭神：[[えびす|恵美須大神]] * 九重供養塔（伝小松内府平重盛卿供養塔） * [[平重盛]]之城址 - 当社の西南「伏松」という小高い丘が城趾と伝承されている。 * [[尼崎藩]]領界碑 - 石標。「従是東尼崎領」など刻銘。 Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|「岡太社」社号標 Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|恵比須社 Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|九重供養塔（伝平重盛供養塔） Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|平重盛之城趾碑 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|尼崎藩領界碑 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|猪像（吽形） 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|猪像（阿形） == 祭礼 == === 一時上臈 === 毎年[[10月11日]]には、南北の講である「頭屋」が男女のヒトガタである[[御幣]]を作り、[[神饌]]とともに奉納される『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.51。。かつては頭屋から神社まで奉納行列が行われていたが、新住民の流入でコミュニティが希薄化し、さらには[[阪神・淡路大震災]]がとどめを刺した形となり『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.54。、平成9年を最後に奉納行列は行われなくなった。 [[江戸時代]]には、その年に小松へ嫁いだ女性の衣装を男が着て供物を奉納する形で行われていたというが、これに付随して[[岩見重太郎]]が[[狒々]]を退治したとする伝説が残っている『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.53。。 === 静止打 === [[1月9日 (旧暦)|陰暦正月9日]]に行われていた。この日は[[西宮神社]]の[[ヒルコ|蛭児神]]が静止（しし）を打つ{{refnest|group="*"|「災害を事前に防ぎ、五穀豊穣をもたらす」の意『[[#鳴尾村誌|鳴尾村誌 1889-1951]]』、p.73。。}}日であり、それを避けるために[[等覚寺 (西宮市)|等覚寺]]へ逃れた[[イノシシ|猪]]（しし）を迎えに行く祭礼であった。のちに途絶しているが、神社の境内には、現在も猪の像が鎮座している。 == 文化財 == === 西宮市指定文化財 === * 無形民俗文化財 ** 岡太神社一時上臈 - 1984年（昭和59年）3月23日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf 西宮市内の指定文化財一覧]}}（西宮市ホームページ）。。 == 交通アクセス == * [[武庫川駅|阪神武庫川駅]]より、北西へ徒歩約6分 * 旧国道（[[中国街道]]）に面する。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="*"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|1=和書|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=西宮市鳴尾区有財産管理委員会|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]]

[[Category:new pages]][[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[Category:shortest 250 pages as of Feb 24]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]][[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]][[Category:qq]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Okata Jinja (岡太神社) | Image = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5年, 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine ranking system明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6年, 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinomiya City]] [[Category:History of Nishinomiya City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Kensha in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6年, 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinomiya City]] [[Category:History of Nishinomiya City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Kensha in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

[[Category:new pages]][[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]][[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]][[Category:qq]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Okata Jinja (岡太神社) | Image = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5年, 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine ranking system明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6年, 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinomiya City]] [[Category:History of Nishinomiya City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Kensha in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) Ronsha

It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5年, 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine ranking system明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6年, 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinomiya City]] [[Category:History of Nishinomiya City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Kensha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}

Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5年, 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine ranking system明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. [[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]][[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]][[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]][[Category:Pages to be written]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Okata Jinja (岡太神社) | Image = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5年, 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine ranking system明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6年, 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} [[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be written]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}

Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}[[Category:Pages to be written]]

revision.text:
[[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]][[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Okata Jinja (岡太神社) | image = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5年, 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine ranking system明治12年調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6年, 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}[[Category:Pages to be written]]

In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}

[[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]][[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Okata Jinja (岡太神社) | image = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5年, 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine ranking system明治12nd year 調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6年, 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}

In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}
