revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Okata Jinja (岡太神社) | image = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5th year , 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine ranking system明治12nd year 調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6th year , 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}

Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神|12=draft}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Okata Jinja (岡太神社) | image = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|12=draft|12=draft}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日|12=draft|12=draft}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神|12=draft|12=draft}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5th year , 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine ranking system明治12nd year 調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6th year , 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] }} [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}

It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神|12=draft|12=draft}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5th year , 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine ranking system明治12nd year 調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6th year , 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] {{citation needed|おかたしんしや}} {{citation needed|Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}

It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine ranking system. Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Okata Jinja (岡太神社) | image = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|12=draft|12=draft}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日|12=draft|12=draft}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine Ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神|12=draft|12=draft}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5th year , 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine Ranking system明治12nd year 調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6th year , 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] {{citation needed|おかたしんしや}} {{citation needed|Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|蘇民将来|" to "|蘇民将来|1=Somin Shōrai|"

Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] {{citation needed|おかたしんしや}} {{citation needed|Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}}

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Okata Jinja (岡太神社) | image = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine Ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来|1=Somin Shōrai}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5th year , 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine Ranking system明治12nd year 調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6th year , 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] {{citation needed|おかたしんしや}} {{citation needed|Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{translated page|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|version=77218964|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|おかたしんしや}} {{citation needed|Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{translated page|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|version=77218964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] }} [[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]] [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Okata Jinja (岡太神社) | image = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|12=draft|12=draft}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日|12=draft|12=draft}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine Ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神|12=draft|12=draft}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来|1=Somin Shōrai|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5th year , 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine Ranking system明治12nd year 調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6th year , 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|おかたしんしや}} {{citation needed|Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{translated page|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|version=77218964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] }} [[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]] [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]]

Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine Ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来|1=Somin Shōrai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5th year , 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine Ranking system明治12nd year 調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6th year , 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|おかたしんしや}} {{citation needed|Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)}} {{translated page|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|version=77218964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] }} [[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]] [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]]

On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|version=77218964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] }} [[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]] [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Okata Jinja (岡太神社) | image = [[File:Okata-jinja, torii.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Grounds Torii (with boar statues on both sides) | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Komatsu Minami-chō 2-chōme 2-8 | Position = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|17.2|N|135|22|45.2|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (small) Ronsha
Formerly a {{ill|Kensha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Founded = | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Okashi no Miya (岡司宮【おかしの宮】) | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|October 11|ja|10月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[#Ichijō Jōraku]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=17.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=45.2|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=岡太神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Okata-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Entrance to the Shrine Grounds}}]] '''Okata Jinja''' (岡太神社【おかたじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Komatsu Minami-chō, Nishinomiya City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a Ronsha-class Shikinaisha, formerly classified as a Kensha under the modern shrine Ranking system. In the Naruo area, it is said to be the oldest shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.46.. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami|ja|天御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Side Enshrinements === * {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|稲田姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Somin-no-Sorai|ja|蘇民将来|1=Somin Shōrai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == According to shrine legend, in the 5th year of Kanpei (寛平5th year , 893) during the reign of Emperor Uda, a man from Mukogun, Hirota – from the Okashi Shin-Go clan – developed this land, calling it Hamamura, and enshrined Hirota Myōjin in the first year of Engi (延喜元年, 901), thereby securing a bountiful harvest『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.47.. * In Meiji 5 (1872) – The northern original five shrines of the main shrine were enshrined together式内社研究会『式内社調査報告』5 (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. * In Meiji 6 (1873) August – Classified as a Sonsha (village shrine). * In Meiji 12 (1879) April – Promoted to Kensha (prefectural shrine) under the modern shrine Ranking system明治12nd year 調べ「神社明細帳」（『西宮市史』第7巻収録 {{全国書誌番号|50007277}}）. * In Heisei 7 (1995), January 17 – The shrine building collapsed in the aftermath of the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * In Heisei 12 (2000) – Reconstructed. === Alternate Names === The shrine is also known as "Okashi no Miya." Several theories have been proposed regarding this epithet: * As mentioned above, it derives from the Okashi (岡司) clan, who founded the shrine『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.48.. * It may have been transferred from Oshōgū, which corresponds to Mukogyū-no-miya, although the designated site for Mukogyū-no-miya is likely elsewhere『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, pp.49–50.. * Alternatively, it is said that a festival in which participants, dressed in women's attire, offered offerings led to the shrine being described as "okashi" (meaning "strange" or "amusing")『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.52.. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Okata-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine Building}}]] * Shrine Name Inscription Stone – Inscribed with "Okata Sha," "Komatsu-mura," and "Dōshi Kan Hirofusa." Built in the 6th year of Genbun (元文6th year , 1741). ** According to Narikawa Seisho, in Kyouhō 19 (1734) he recorded in the "Gokinin-shi" section of the "Settsu-shi" in "Gokinin-shi" that "Okata Jinja is located in Komatsu-mura," and installed the inscription stone, thereby identifying it as a Shikinaisha. Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|version=77218964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] }} [[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]] [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

Another Ronsha, Okata Jinja located on the grounds of Kobe Jogakuin University (Okata Jinja in Higashihime), is also considered a Ronsha. Since both shrines enshrine Ame-no-Minakanushi Omikami, some believe there is a connection between the two『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.49.. * Ebisu-sha – Located in the left rear of the shrine building. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu Ōkami|ja|恵美須大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * The Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally identified as the memorial pagoda for Heichimasa Taira Shigemori). * The site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s Castle – A small hill called "Fusamatsu" in the southwest of the shrine is traditionally regarded as the site of his castle. * Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone – A stone marker inscribed with phrases such as "Accordingly, under the Amagasaki Domain" etc. Okata-jinja, sekihi-1.jpg|Inscription stone of "Okata Sha" Okata-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu-sha Okata-jinja, tou.jpg|Kokonjū Memorial Pagoda (traditionally for Heichimasa Shigemori) Okata-jinja, sekihi-2.jpg|Stone monument at the site of Heichimasa Shigemori’s castle 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Amagasaki-han Ryokai.jpg|Amagasaki Domain Boundary Stone 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi Un.jpg|Boar statue (Un-type) 2016 0521 Okata-jinja Nishinomiya Shishi A.jpg|Boar statue (A-type) == Festivals == === Ichijō Jōraku (一時上臈) === Every year on October 11, a ceremony called "Atariya" is held in which a pair of human-shaped ritual implements (【御幣】) are crafted by both the northern and southern groups and offered along with divine food (【神饌】)『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.51.. In the past, a procession carrying these offerings from the Atariya to the shrine was held, but due to an influx of new residents and a consequent weakening of community bonds—not to mention the final blow delivered by the Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake—the procession was discontinued after Heisei 9. During the Edo period, it is said that on that day, men would dress in the attire of women who had married into Komatsu, and offer sacrifices; a legend also recounts that Iwami Jūtarō defeated a ferocious beast (狒々) in connection with this ritual. === Seishida (静止打) === Held on the 9th day of the lunar New Year (陰暦正月9日). On this day, the Hiruji-no-Kami (蛭児神) of Nishinomiya Jinja is said to perform a ritual called "Seishi" (静止打), which is intended "to prevent disasters in advance and ensure a rich harvest"『鳴尾村誌 1889-1951』, p.73.. To avoid this day, a festival was held in which a boar (猪) was sent to fetch participants from Tōgakudera in Nishinomiya. Although this ritual later ceased, boar statues still remain within the shrine grounds. == Cultural Properties == === Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property: ** Okata Jinja Ichijō Jōraku – Designated on March 23, 1984 (Shōwa 59){{PDFlink|[https://www.nishi.or.jp/bunka/rekishitobunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai-hogo.files/20200316nishinomiya_shiteiunkazai.pdf Nishinomiya City Designated Cultural Properties List]}} == Access == * From [[Hanshin Mukogawa Station|Hanshin Mukogawa Station]] – Approximately 6 minutes on foot to the northwest * Facing the old national highway (【中国街道】). == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|year=2005|title=鳴尾村誌 1885-1951|publisher=West Nishinomiya Naruo Property Management Committee|editor=鳴尾村誌編纂委員会|ref=鳴尾村誌}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Okata-jinja}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]]\n{{translated page|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|version=77218964|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] }} [[sn:Okata Shrine (Nishinomiya)]] [[ja:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[sn:岡太神社 (西宮市)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]
